title: [Program] Local Real-Time Transcription v1 on Sequoia Capture
description:
Umbrella initiative for shipping a fully local, low-latency transcription stack on top of current ScreenCaptureKit capture. This issue anchors goals, sequencing, and decision quality thresholds so implementation remains measurable instead of ad hoc.

## Design
Use the validated capture baseline as the platform foundation, then layer transcription in phases gated by benchmark evidence. Treat latency, reliability, and local-only privacy as first-class requirements, not afterthoughts.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) A complete dependency graph exists for all major workstreams. 2) Each phase has explicit entry/exit criteria and measurable outputs. 3) Program-level SLOs are represented in child issues and benchmarks. 4) First-run success is prioritized: operators can diagnose environment readiness before recording/transcribing. 5) Transcript outputs are readable and trustworthy by default, with deterministic formatting and clear degradation signaling.

## Notes
This issue is the canonical intent record. Child epics should not drift from local-first privacy, benchmark-driven decisions, and macOS 15+ scope unless this parent is explicitly updated.
design: Use the validated capture baseline as the platform foundation, then layer transcription in phases gated by benchmark evidence. Treat latency, reliability, and local-only privacy as first-class requirements, not afterthoughts.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) A complete dependency graph exists for all major workstreams. 2) Each phase has explicit entry/exit criteria and measurable outputs. 3) Program-level SLOs are represented in child issues and benchmarks. 4) First-run success is prioritized: operators can diagnose environment readiness before recording/transcribing. 5) Transcript outputs are readable and trustworthy by default, with deterministic formatting and clear degradation signaling.
notes: This issue is the canonical intent record. Child epics should not drift from local-first privacy, benchmark-driven decisions, and macOS 15+ scope unless this parent is explicitly updated.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Reasoning context: We already proved stereo capture viability (mic/system via ScreenCaptureKit). The remaining risk is execution discipline: model selection, latency control, and operational hardening. This program issue preserves that discipline and links all derived decisions to benchmark evidence.

title: [Program] Local Real-Time Transcription v1 on Sequoia Capture
description: Program-level umbrella for delivering fully-local low-latency transcription on top of the existing ScreenCaptureKit capture baseline, with measurable SLOs and reproducible artifacts.

title: [A1] Introduce transcribe-live CLI and configuration contract
description:
Create the main user entrypoint for live transcription with coherent flags for duration, outputs, backend selection, and processing modes.

## Design
Align command semantics with existing capture binaries and Makefile patterns to reduce operator friction.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) command parses and validates key flags. 2) run configuration is printed clearly. 3) failure states are actionable and consistent.

## Notes
This task establishes interface stability so internals can evolve safely.
design: Align command semantics with existing capture binaries and Makefile patterns to reduce operator friction.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) command parses and validates key flags. 2) run configuration is printed clearly. 3) failure states are actionable and consistent.
notes: This task establishes interface stability so internals can evolve safely.
close_reason: Completed: added transcribe-live CLI contract parser/validator and README contract docs

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: stable operator contract first, internal iteration second.

title: [DOC3] Publish beads governance guide for decomposition and traceability
description:
Document conventions for issue decomposition, dependency mapping, acceptance quality, and decision traceability in this project.

## Design
Include guidance for linking evidence, using labels, and escalating design decisions into ADRs.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) governance doc defines hierarchy and dependency practices. 2) evidence-linking conventions are explicit. 3) onboarding path is clear for new contributors/agents.

## Notes
This task sustains roadmap coherence as contributor count and context turnover increase.

notes: This task sustains roadmap coherence as contributor count and context turnover increase.

## Notes

title: [DU1] Implement concurrent mic/system workers and deterministic merge
description:
Run channel-specific ASR workers concurrently and merge results using deterministic timestamp alignment.

## Design
Preserve channel attribution and deterministic ordering for identical inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) both channels process concurrently without starvation. 2) merge output is deterministic. 3) channel labels persist in merged transcript metadata.

## Notes
This task delivers core dual-channel functionality and preserves semantic source fidelity.
design: Preserve channel attribution and deterministic ordering for identical inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) both channels process concurrently without starvation. 2) merge output is deterministic. 3) channel labels persist in merged transcript metadata.
notes: This task delivers core dual-channel functionality and preserves semantic source fidelity.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: channel provenance is a key product value, not an implementation detail.

title: [UX1] Add transcribe-live preflight diagnostics for first-run success
description:
Add a preflight diagnostics mode that checks the full local runtime prerequisites before capture/transcription starts, with explicit pass/fail output and remediation hints.

## Design
Preflight should verify display availability, ScreenCapture/Microphone permission state, model path availability, writable artifact directories, and expected sample-rate constraints. Output should be deterministic and script-friendly.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) transcribe-live --preflight (or equivalent command) prints a structured checklist with PASS/WARN/FAIL states. 2) each FAIL includes one-step remediation guidance. 3) preflight runs in debug and signed-app contexts and records result in session manifest.

## Notes
This task directly improves user time-to-first-success and lowers support burden by turning opaque startup failures into actionable checks.
design: Preflight should verify display availability, ScreenCapture/Microphone permission state, model path availability, writable artifact directories, and expected sample-rate constraints. Output should be deterministic and script-friendly.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) transcribe-live --preflight (or equivalent command) prints a structured checklist with PASS/WARN/FAIL states. 2) each FAIL includes one-step remediation guidance. 3) preflight runs in debug and signed-app contexts and records result in session manifest.
notes: This task directly improves user time-to-first-success and lowers support burden by turning opaque startup failures into actionable checks.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: User-centric reasoning: the fastest way to improve real user outcomes is reducing setup uncertainty before runtime. Preflight catches common environmental and permission errors early.

## Acceptance Criteria

## Notes

title: [Epic] Finalized-Segment LLM Cleanup (Phase 3)
description:
Add optional local LLM cleanup for finalized segments only, with strict non-blocking guarantees on the ASR critical path.

## Design
Use queue-isolated post-processing to improve readability without compromising real-time transcription continuity.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) cleanup queue/backpressure policy exists. 2) llm_final events are emitted when cleanup succeeds. 3) ASR remains healthy when LLM is slow or unavailable.

## Notes
This epic is additive quality enhancement and must never degrade core transcription behavior.
design: Use queue-isolated post-processing to improve readability without compromising real-time transcription continuity.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) cleanup queue/backpressure policy exists. 2) llm_final events are emitted when cleanup succeeds. 3) ASR remains healthy when LLM is slow or unavailable.
notes: This epic is additive quality enhancement and must never degrade core transcription behavior.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Context note: PLAN.md explicitly calls for asynchronous cleanup and skip/drop behavior under queue pressure.

title: [C3] Add drop telemetry and callback contract audit instrumentation
description:
Instrument callback and transport behavior to quantify drops, occupancy pressure, and contract violations.

## Design
Expose counters that help separate upstream capture starvation from downstream ASR bottlenecks.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) drop and occupancy metrics are emitted. 2) callback contract checks detect forbidden behaviors. 3) telemetry is included in run artifacts.

## Notes
This task enables evidence-based tuning rather than guesswork.
design: Expose counters that help separate upstream capture starvation from downstream ASR bottlenecks.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) drop and occupancy metrics are emitted. 2) callback contract checks detect forbidden behaviors. 3) telemetry is included in run artifacts.
notes: This task enables evidence-based tuning rather than guesswork.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: observability before optimization is mandatory for real-time systems.

title: [D2] Execute Gate A and Gate B experiments
description:
Run formal model selection and integration viability experiments to validate both backend performance and Rust integration overhead.

## Design
Gate A compares candidate ASR backends under identical workloads. Gate B validates Rust invocation overhead and repeated-run stability.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) Gate A report includes comparative metrics and recommendation. 2) Gate B report includes overhead ratio and failure analysis. 3) artifacts are committed in benchmark evidence paths.

## Notes
This task prevents expensive lock-in on attractive but operationally weak backend options.
design: Gate A compares candidate ASR backends under identical workloads. Gate B validates Rust invocation overhead and repeated-run stability.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) Gate A report includes comparative metrics and recommendation. 2) Gate B report includes overhead ratio and failure analysis. 3) artifacts are committed in benchmark evidence paths.
notes: This task prevents expensive lock-in on attractive but operationally weak backend options.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: model quality and integration practicality must both pass for a valid choice.

title: [Epic] Discovery Gates and Backend Decision (A/B)
description:
Run evidence-first discovery gates to choose the ASR backend and integration approach that can hit latency and stability targets on Apple Silicon.

## Design
Gate A compares backend candidates under identical corpus and hardware constraints. Gate B validates Rust integration overhead and runtime stability before deep implementation investment.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) Benchmark harness and corpus are reproducible. 2) Gate A and Gate B outputs are archived. 3) ADR-001 records final backend choice and fallback plan.

## Notes
Avoid architecture lock-in based on unverified assumptions. Discovery artifacts are the authority for downstream design decisions.
design: Gate A compares backend candidates under identical corpus and hardware constraints. Gate B validates Rust integration overhead and runtime stability before deep implementation investment.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) Benchmark harness and corpus are reproducible. 2) Gate A and Gate B outputs are archived. 3) ADR-001 records final backend choice and fallback plan.
notes: Avoid architecture lock-in based on unverified assumptions. Discovery artifacts are the authority for downstream design decisions.

title: [Epic] RT Safety, Telemetry, and Soak Stability (Phase 4)
description:
Enforce real-time safety contracts and gather telemetry needed to prove long-session reliability by evidence instead of assumption.

## Design
Add counters, high-water marks, recovery visibility, and reproducible soak procedures to detect silent degradation over time.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) queue/drop/latency telemetry is available in artifacts. 2) 60-minute soak criteria are defined and executed. 3) regression gate commands are repeatable.

## Notes
This epic converts prototype confidence into operational confidence.
design: Add counters, high-water marks, recovery visibility, and reproducible soak procedures to detect silent degradation over time.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) queue/drop/latency telemetry is available in artifacts. 2) 60-minute soak criteria are defined and executed. 3) regression gate commands are repeatable.
notes: This epic converts prototype confidence into operational confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria

This task reduces deployment fragility as model artifacts become required runtime dependencies.

title: [D1] Build benchmark harness and canonical evaluation corpus
description:
Create repeatable benchmark tooling and corpus conventions so latency and quality comparisons stay consistent across backends and runs.

## Design
Define fixed input corpus, hardware metadata capture, and output schema including latency p50/p95, CPU, RSS, and error counters.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) one command executes benchmark and emits machine-readable artifact. 2) corpus definition and versioning are documented. 3) harness output includes timestamp and commit linkage.

## Notes
Without this task, downstream performance claims remain anecdotal and cannot be compared reliably.
design: Define fixed input corpus, hardware metadata capture, and output schema including latency p50/p95, CPU, RSS, and error counters.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) one command executes benchmark and emits machine-readable artifact. 2) corpus definition and versioning are documented. 3) harness output includes timestamp and commit linkage.

title: [C1] Implement preallocated lock-free callback transport
description:
Move callback-path delivery to deterministic preallocated lock-free transport aligned with the architecture contract.

## Design
Implement fixed-capacity chunk slots and SPSC ring semantics for predictable behavior under sustained load.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) callback thread performs no blocking I/O. 2) transport capacity and overflow behavior are explicit. 3) transport behavior is benchmarked under stress.

## Notes
This task is the structural foundation for real-time safety and predictable upstream data flow.
design: Implement fixed-capacity chunk slots and SPSC ring semantics for predictable behavior under sustained load.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) callback thread performs no blocking I/O. 2) transport capacity and overflow behavior are explicit. 3) transport behavior is benchmarked under stress.
notes: This task is the structural foundation for real-time safety and predictable upstream data flow.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: code and docs currently differ on transport strategy; this closes that gap.

title: [Epic] Dual-Channel Concurrent ASR and Merge (Phase 2)
description:
Run independent transcription pipelines for mic and system channels, then merge results by timeline into coherent transcript outputs.

## Design
Preserve channel fidelity while providing a mixed fallback mode for constrained hardware or saturation scenarios.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) mic/system transcribe concurrently. 2) PTS-based merge is deterministic. 3) Gate C stress results validate or bound dual-channel viability.

## Notes
This epic bridges technical correctness (two streams) and practical utility (one coherent transcript narrative).
design: Preserve channel fidelity while providing a mixed fallback mode for constrained hardware or saturation scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) mic/system transcribe concurrently. 2) PTS-based merge is deterministic. 3) Gate C stress results validate or bound dual-channel viability.
notes: This epic bridges technical correctness (two streams) and practical utility (one coherent transcript narrative).

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Context note: channel separation is already a core capture invariant; this epic preserves that semantic value through ASR.

title: [UX2] Improve transcript readability defaults (timestamps, speaker tags, overlap policy)
description:
Define and implement user-readable transcript defaults that preserve technical correctness while making raw outputs usable without post-editing.

## Design
Establish a stable output format for timestamps, channel/speaker tags, segment boundaries, and overlap handling so transcripts are understandable in real time and in saved artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) merged and per-channel outputs follow a documented default formatting contract. 2) overlap/near-simultaneous speech has deterministic presentation rules. 3) sample outputs are included in docs and validated in replay tests.

## Notes
This task converts technically correct transcripts into practically useful transcripts for end users reading and acting on them.
design: Establish a stable output format for timestamps, channel/speaker tags, segment boundaries, and overlap handling so transcripts are understandable in real time and in saved artifacts.

title: [R4] Make ScreenCaptureKit test commands resolve Swift runtime without manual DYLD export

2) failure mode is clearly documented for contributors/agents.
3) CI/local command examples are consistent.

title: [L3] Benchmark cleanup latency/quality and codify disable thresholds
description:
Measure cleanup performance and readability gain on representative segment sets, then define policy thresholds for automatic degradation.

## Design
Focus on net operator value under local-only constraints, not raw model capability.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) benchmark artifact includes cleanup p50/p95 and queue impact. 2) quality rubric and sample evaluation are documented. 3) auto-disable thresholds are explicit.

## Notes
This task prevents cosmetic quality work from silently consuming reliability budget.
design: Focus on net operator value under local-only constraints, not raw model capability.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) benchmark artifact includes cleanup p50/p95 and queue impact. 2) quality rubric and sample evaluation are documented. 3) auto-disable thresholds are explicit.
notes: This task prevents cosmetic quality work from silently consuming reliability budget.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: optional features must justify their cost with measured evidence.

title: [L1] Implement async cleanup queue with strict ASR isolation
description:
Build queue-isolated cleanup processing so finalized text enhancement never blocks transcription critical paths.

## Design
Define queue depth, timeout, retry, and drop semantics with explicit observability hooks.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) ASR remains healthy when cleanup stalls. 2) queue policy is configurable and documented. 3) cleanup failures are visible in telemetry and artifacts.

## Notes
This task enforces the principle that cleanup is optional enhancement, never required for core operation.
design: Define queue depth, timeout, retry, and drop semantics with explicit observability hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) ASR remains healthy when cleanup stalls. 2) queue policy is configurable and documented. 3) cleanup failures are visible in telemetry and artifacts.
notes: This task enforces the principle that cleanup is optional enhancement, never required for core operation.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: performance isolation protects primary SLOs from secondary feature variance.

title: [DOC2] Define session manifest schema and benchmark interpretation guide
description:
Define artifact schemas and interpretation guidance so future maintainers can evaluate run outcomes without re-deriving context.

## Design
Document field semantics for config, counters, outputs, and outcome states.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) manifest schema is versioned. 2) example artifacts are provided. 3) benchmark interpretation guide includes pass/fail caveats and comparison advice.

## Notes
This task turns raw output into durable, reusable project knowledge.
design: Document field semantics for config, counters, outputs, and outcome states.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) manifest schema is versioned. 2) example artifacts are provided. 3) benchmark interpretation guide includes pass/fail caveats and comparison advice.
notes: This task turns raw output into durable, reusable project knowledge.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: metrics are only useful if later readers can interpret them consistently.

title: [C2] Define and implement interruption and sample-rate recovery policy
description:
Handle non-happy-path capture explicitly, including stopped stream recovery and deterministic sample-rate mismatch behavior.

## Design
Define fail-fast versus fallback behavior with clear diagnostics so operators and developers can reason about outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) interruption recovery path is tested. 2) sample-rate mismatch behavior is deterministic. 3) operator-facing errors are actionable.

## Notes
This task prevents transient capture failures from cascading into opaque transcription instability.
design: Define fail-fast versus fallback behavior with clear diagnostics so operators and developers can reason about outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) interruption recovery path is tested. 2) sample-rate mismatch behavior is deterministic. 3) operator-facing errors are actionable.
notes: This task prevents transient capture failures from cascading into opaque transcription instability.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: explicit recovery policy is cheaper than ad hoc troubleshooting.

title: [A3] Emit partial/final JSONL events, add replay path, and validate single-channel SLOs
description:
Build structured event outputs and replayability, then run single-channel benchmark for declared latency targets.

## Design
Provide a data trail that can be replayed and inspected for correctness, timing, and regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) partial/final events stream to terminal and JSONL. 2) replay reconstructs transcript timeline. 3) single-channel benchmark artifact reports p50/p95 latency.

## Notes
This task turns transcription from an opaque stream into a measurable artifact pipeline.
design: Provide a data trail that can be replayed and inspected for correctness, timing, and regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) partial/final events stream to terminal and JSONL. 2) replay reconstructs transcript timeline. 3) single-channel benchmark artifact reports p50/p95 latency.
notes: This task turns transcription from an opaque stream into a measurable artifact pipeline.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Rationale: replayability is required for fast debugging and regression detection.

title: [Epic] Capture Pipeline Hardening for Real-Time
description:
Harden the existing capture path so it can serve as a dependable upstream for live transcription under load and long session durations.

## Design
Move from prototype transport behavior to explicit callback-path constraints, robust recovery, and observability for drops and backpressure.

## Success Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) Callback-path constraints are enforced and measured. 2) Recovery behavior is defined for stream interruptions. 3) Transport/sample-rate assumptions are explicit and tested.

## Notes
Transcription quality and latency are downstream of capture integrity; this epic protects system reliability at the source.
design: Move from prototype transport behavior to explicit callback-path constraints, robust recovery, and observability for drops and backpressure.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) Callback-path constraints are enforced and measured. 2) Recovery behavior is defined for stream interruptions. 3) Transport/sample-rate assumptions are explicit and tested.
notes: Transcription quality and latency are downstream of capture integrity; this epic protects system reliability at the source.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: Context note: current code is strong prototype quality but still needs production-grade transport and telemetry guarantees.

title: [UX3] Add explicit degraded-mode notices and trust signals in live output
description:
When the pipeline degrades (fallback modes, dropped chunks, skipped cleanup), surface this clearly to users in real time and in persisted artifacts so transcript trust remains calibrated.

## Design
Implement a unified user-notification policy for degradation events across terminal output and session artifacts, including cause, impact, and next-step guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria: 1) degraded-mode transitions emit visible terminal notices with concise impact statements. 2) session manifests include structured degradation/trust metadata. 3) replayed transcripts preserve notice context for auditability.

## Notes
This task improves user trust by preventing silent quality regressions from being mistaken for authoritative transcript truth.
design: Implement a unified user-notification policy for degradation events across terminal output and session artifacts, including cause, impact, and next-step guidance.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria: 1) degraded-mode transitions emit visible terminal notices with concise impact statements. 2) session manifests include structured degradation/trust metadata. 3) replayed transcripts preserve notice context for auditability.
notes: This task improves user trust by preventing silent quality regressions from being mistaken for authoritative transcript truth.

comments.author: HoangNB
comments.text: User-centric reasoning: users need calibrated trust, not false confidence. Clear degradation signals protect downstream decisions made from transcript output.
